records.ety: [L. successus: cf. F. succs. See Succeed.]

quotes: Then all the sons of these five brethren reigned By due success.

defs: That which comes after; hence, consequence, issue, or result, of an endeavor or undertaking, whether good or bad; the outcome of effort.
quotes: Men . . . that are like to do that, that is committed to them, and to report back again faithfully the success.
Perplexed and troubled at his bad success The tempter stood.

defs: The favorable or prosperous termination of anything attempted; the attainment of a proposed object; prosperous issue.
quotes: Dream of success and happy victory!
Or teach with more success her son The vices of the time to shun.
Military successes, above all others, elevate the minds of a people.

defs: That which meets with, or one who accomplishes, favorable results, as a play or a player.
